windows.transcript: most important when it comes to lifetime quality of your candle. Burn the candle long enough that the melted wax reaches the edge of the container.
windows.embed_text_v2: Lighting wooden wick container candle with butane lighter, then burn shots of amber glass jar candle and tealight, overlay arrows pointing to melt pool edge, single flame, smooth white and amber wax, finishing-product burn instruction footage.
windows.summary_v2: A wooden-wick container candle is lit and shown burning, with overlay arrows illustrating proper melt-pool reach to the jar edge.

tags_v2.action: lighting and demonstrating burn pool of finished candle

seconds_v2.desc: Close on a white pillar-style jar candle as a long-neck butane lighter ignites a wide wooden wick; flame leans toward the lighter.
Wooden wick now alight on the white candle; lighter held to the wick edge, small flame growing.
Lighter still angled at the wooden wick, flame slightly larger, wax surface smooth and white.
Lighter held in place as the wooden wick takes hold of the flame.
Top-down view of an amber jar candle with single lit wick centered; two orange curved arrow graphics overlay pointing inward toward the wax pool edge.
Same top-down lit candle with overlay arrows highlighting the desired melt pool reaching the container edge.
Cut to soft-focus tealight burning in the background, transitioning to next shot.
Lit amber glass jar candle with single flame, slight melt pool forming around centered wick.
Continued close-up of amber jar candle with steady single flame; wax surface around the wick illuminated.
